title: Chest X-Ray Multi-Task Research
subtitle: Telplastina: foundational multi-task CXR platform for clinical AI-assisted diagnosis

milestones.role: Researcher · Classification and Segmentation Module Lead
highlights: Recruited and onboarded team members to join the multi-task CXR research project

Pathology classification module: fine-tuned TorchXRayVision DenseNet121 on VinBig (14 labels) and NIH ChestX-ray14 (14 labels); input center-cropped to 224x224 in XRV-native intensity range, probabilities via sigmoid
Also trained DeiT-based multi-label classification models on standard CXR benchmarks for comparison
Fine-tuned TorchXRayVision pretrained weights for domain adaptation to the project's target dataset

Organ segmentation module: TorchXRayVision PSPNet segmenting 3 structures — right lung (idx 5), left lung (idx 4), and heart (idx 8); square-padded to native scale, thresholded at 0.5, outputs binary masks, bounding boxes, and colored overlay PNG with DICOM support via pydicom
Produced AI inference modules for the FastAPI backend, consumed by both web (React + Next.js) and desktop (Tauri v2) frontends
System features an interactive ReportCard with doctor-editable findings and impressions, probability bars, and a canvas-based Grounding Editor for organ annotation using an offscreen mask pattern
